venues.name: All American Club

capacity_source.method: venue-stated (Eventective listing: Max Number of People for an Event: 175)

venues.notable: Longtime West End neighborhood bar, restaurant, and reception hall known for catering and BBQ. Situated in the Lincoln Park / West End area of Duluth.

business.source: City of Duluth annual on-sale club licensee list (Legistar PDF)
business.note: Listed on Duluth's annual licensee roster as ALL AMERICAN CLUB. No city general business license number published in available records; Duluth issues hospitality/liquor licenses through the City Clerk.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: ALL AMERICAN CLUB

venues.name: Amsoil Arena
venues.notable: Multipurpose arena attached to the Duluth Entertainment Convention Center (DECC) on the waterfront near the Aerial Lift Bridge. Home to UMD Bulldogs men's and women's hockey. Seats ~6,600 for hockey and ~8,500 for concerts. Opened December 2010, replacing the older DECC Arena.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); DECC is a city-operated authority
business.note: DECC/Amsoil Arena is operated by the Duluth Entertainment Convention Center, a city authority. Listed as DULUTH ENTERTAINMENT CONVENTION CENTER on Duluth's on-sale intoxicating license list at 350 Harbor Drive.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: DULUTH ENTERTAINMENT CONVENTION CENTER

venues.name: Average Joe's Pub
venues.notable: Neighborhood sports bar in Superior's North End, covered by the Superior Telegram as a new pub when it opened.

business.source: Wisconsin DOR Retail Alcohol Beverage License Search API (ww2.revenue.wi.gov/WebServicesPublicWeb/rest/liquor/all); legal entity: SUPERIOR JOES DEC LLC
business.note: DBA listed as AVERAGE JOE'S in WI DOR database. License expires 2026-06-30. City of Superior business license is issued by the City Clerk; individual license number not published in state database.

liquor.license_type: Class B Beer + Class B Liquor (combination)
liquor.source: Wisconsin DOR Retail Alcohol Beverage License Search; legal entity: SUPERIOR JOES DEC LLC; lastUpdated: 2026-03-26; expireDate: 2026-06-30

venues.name: Bent Paddle Brewing Company Taproom

capacity_source.method: venue-stated (full taproom rental page: 140 seated / 200 standing, plus heated patio)

venues.notable: Flagship craft brewery and anchor of the Lincoln Park Craft District. Founded in 2013; the expanded taproom opened in Lincoln Park after the brewery outgrew its original West Michigan Street space. Known for Black Ale, Venture Pils, and Cold Press Black.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: BENT PADDLE BREWING CO.
business.note: Listed under Breweries/Distilleries/Cideries on Duluth's annual licensee roster.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: BENT PADDLE BREWING CO.

venues.name: Blacklist Brewing Company
venues.notable: Downtown Duluth craft brewery known for Belgian-inspired and experimental ales. Originally founded in Duluth in 2012; moved to the current East Superior Street taproom after expanding from earlier spaces.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: BLACKLIST BEER, LLC; DBA: BLACKLIST ARTISAN ALES

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: BLACKLIST BEER, LLC; DBA: BLACKLIST ARTISAN ALES

venues.name: Caddy Shack Indoor Golf & Pub
venues.notable: Duluth's only indoor golf simulator bar and pub, located in the Lincoln Park Craft District on West Superior Street.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: CRAZY UNCLE ENTERTAINMENT; DBA: CADDY SHACK-DULUTH INDOOR GOLF & PUB; address: 2023 W SUPERIOR ST

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: CRAZY UNCLE ENTERTAINMENT

venues.name: Canal Park Brewing Company
venues.notable: Lakeside brewpub with patio views of Lake Superior, located across from the Lakewalk in Canal Park. Opened 2012.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: CANAL PARK BREWING CO., LLC; address: 300 CANAL PARK DR

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list; listed under both On-Sale Intoxicating and Brewery On/Off Sale; legal entity: CANAL PARK BREWING CO., LLC

venues.name: Carmody Irish Pub
venues.notable: Downtown Duluth Irish pub and brewery, family-owned since 2006. Longtime live-music room with traditional Irish sessions and local rock/folk acts.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: HIPPO DOWN THE STREET, LLC; DBA: CARMODY IRISH PUB; address: 308 E SUPERIOR ST
business.note: Legal entity name differs from DBA: HIPPO DOWN THE STREET, LLC operates Carmody Irish Pub.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list; HIPPO DOWN THE STREET, LLC appears in both On-Sale Intoxicating and Brewery On/Off Sale sections

venues.name: Chester Bowl Park
venues.notable: 117-acre city park with an alpine ski hill (double chairlift, 175-foot drop), historic ski jumps, and a chalet. Managed in part by the Chester Bowl Improvement Club. Hosts outdoor summer concerts and the annual Fall Fest.

business.note: Chester Bowl is a City of Duluth public park managed partly by the Chester Bowl Improvement Club (nonprofit). No city business license record found. Searched duluthmn.gov City Clerk pages and Duluth Legistar; no commercial business license expected for a city park.

liquor.note: City park / nonprofit-managed venue. Not found on Duluth annual liquor licensee list. May obtain temporary on-sale permits for specific events; no permanent license identified.

venues.name: Dovetail Cafe and Marketplace
venues.notable: Cafe and handcraft marketplace run as a partner project of the Duluth Folk School in the Lincoln Park Craft District. Serves as an intimate listening room for acoustic and folk acts.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); listed under On-Sale Wine & Beer as DOVETAIL CAFÉ & MARKETPLACE

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: DOVETAIL CAFÉ & MARKETPLACE

venues.name: Dubh Linn Irish Pub
venues.notable: Downtown Duluth Irish brew pub with a large scotch and whiskey selection, live Irish music, and regular comedy nights.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: DUBH LINN, INC; DBA: DUBH LINN IRISH BREW PUB; address: 109 W SUPERIOR ST

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: DUBH LINN, INC

venues.name: Duluth Cider
venues.notable: Craft cidery and taproom in the Lincoln Park Craft District producing a rotating selection of hard ciders. Opened in 2018.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); listed under Breweries/Distilleries/Cideries as DULUTH CIDER, LLC; DBA: DULUTH CIDER

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: DULUTH CIDER, LLC. April 2026 AGT agenda also shows a DULUTH CIDER (PICKLEFEST) temporary expansion application.

venues.name: Duluth Depot: Great Hall
venues.notable: The Great Hall is the former general waiting room of the 1892 Union Depot (now the St. Louis County Heritage & Arts Center / The Depot). Features an 88-foot ceiling and twin fireplaces; accommodates up to 250 guests. The Depot served seven railroads before closing as a station in 1969.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: HISTORIC UNION DEPOT, INC; DBA: SLC HERITAGE & ARTS CTR; address: 506 W MICHIGAN ST
business.note: The building is officially the St. Louis County Heritage & Arts Center (The Depot), operated by Historic Union Depot, Inc.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: HISTORIC UNION DEPOT, INC

venues.name: Duluth Flame Nightclub

capacity_source.method: venue-stated (venue's own rent-our-space page: upper level 300+, downstairs lounge 100+, combined 400+)

venues.notable: Downtown Duluth nightclub and LGBTQ+ friendly dance club on 1st Ave West near Superior Street. Hosts drag shows, DJ nights, and live acts.

venues.name: Gopher Restaurant & Lounge
venues.notable: Longtime West Duluth neighborhood bar at the corner of Central Ave and Ramsey St, known for wings, Dago Bites, karaoke, and first-Friday live music and comedy. Also listed as Gopher Bar & Grill.

venues.notable: Cuban restaurant and cocktail bar in downtown Superior known for authentic Cuban sandwiches, rum cocktails, and live music nights.

venues.name: Kom-on-Inn
venues.notable: Classic West Duluth neighborhood dive bar famous for its hand-painted interior murals depicting Duluth history and Lake Superior scenes. A Homegrown Music Festival staple.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: JMM LLLP; DBA: KOM ON INN; address: 332 N 57TH AVE W
business.note: Legal entity name differs from DBA: JMM LLLP operates Kom-on-Inn.

venues.name: Lake Superior Zoo
venues.notable: Zoo in Fairmont Park in far West Duluth, founded 1923. Grounds host occasional outdoor concerts and after-hours events.

business.note: Lake Superior Zoo is a city-operated facility. Not found on Duluth annual liquor licensee list. Searched duluthmn.gov and Duluth Legistar; no commercial liquor license expected for a zoo.

liquor.note: No liquor license found. City zoo; may use temporary permits for special events.

venues.name: Legacy Cannabis
venues.notable: Reported as Minnesota's first licensed adult-use cannabis dispensary, operated by the Red Lake Nation in the Lincoln Park Craft District.

business.source: City of Duluth Cannabis Registration program; operated under Red Lake Nation tribal authority with state OCM license
business.note: Legacy Cannabis holds a Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management license as Minnesota's first legal adult-use cannabis retailer. City of Duluth has a Cannabis Registration program (separate from business licensing). No Duluth business license number found in publicly available records.

liquor.note: Cannabis dispensary; no alcohol license held or expected.

venues.name: Mr. D's Bar & Grill
venues.notable: West Duluth neighborhood bar and grill on Grand Avenue, known for live rock and tribute acts.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: D & D ENTERPRISES OF CLOQUET, INC; DBA: MR. D'S SPIRIT VALLEY; address: 5622 GRAND AVE

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: D & D ENTERPRISES OF CLOQUET, INC

venues.name: Norway Hall Event Center

capacity_source.method: venue-stated (Duluth News Tribune: up to 135 for dinner / 285 for cocktails; 285 is stated maximum)

venues.notable: Historic second-floor ballroom at 21 N Lake Ave, reopened as an event center in 2024. The building has Scandinavian fraternal roots; the ballroom is roughly 2,000 square feet and is also operated as Wild Rose Company at Norway Hall.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: WILD ROSE COMPANY LLC
business.note: Norway Hall Event Center is operated by Wild Rose Company LLC, which also operates The Bridge Cocktail Room. Both DBAs appear to share the on-sale intoxicating license. Legal entity: WILD ROSE COMPANY LLC.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: WILD ROSE COMPANY LLC

venues.name: Owls Club
venues.notable: Fraternal organization (Order of Owls) over 115 years old, housed in a former funeral home on the northern edge of downtown Duluth. Private bottle club that has recently seen a surge of young members and become a distinctive Homegrown Festival venue.

business.note: Owls Club is a private fraternal bottle club (members bring their own alcohol; no retail liquor sales). Not found on Duluth's annual on-sale or club liquor licensee list. Searched duluthmn.gov Legistar and both annual licensee PDFs; no record located. Private clubs of this type typically do not hold a public liquor license.

liquor.note: Bottle club — members bring their own alcohol. No retail liquor license held. Not found in Duluth annual licensee list.

venues.name: Pizza Luce
venues.notable: Duluth outpost of the Minneapolis-based Pizza Luce chain. The downtown location has long hosted touring indie and punk acts in its back dining room and bar, and is a perennial Homegrown venue.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: PIZZA LUCE III, INC; DBA: PIZZA LUCE; address: 11 E SUPERIOR ST

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: PIZZA LUCE III, INC

venues.name: Prøve Gallery
venues.notable: Artist-run contemporary art gallery and collective (PRØVE Collective) in downtown Duluth, founded around 2011. Hosts rotating exhibitions, zine projects, and occasional music events.

business.note: Prøve Gallery is a nonprofit artist collective. Not found on Duluth annual liquor licensee list. Searched duluthmn.gov Legistar and annual licensee PDFs. No liquor license expected for an art gallery.

liquor.note: Art gallery / nonprofit collective. No liquor license found. May serve wine at receptions under a temporary permit.

venues.name: R.T. Quinlan's Saloon
venues.notable: Downtown Duluth dive bar and live-music room that has been a community staple for over 30 years, and a long-running Homegrown Festival venue.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: SILVER HAMMER, INC; DBA: RT QUINLAN'S SALOON
business.note: Legal entity name differs from DBA: SILVER HAMMER, INC operates R.T. Quinlan's Saloon.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: SILVER HAMMER, INC

venues.name: Ritual Marketplace
venues.notable: Creative collective and marketplace in Superior's historic Trade and Commerce building on Broadway, opened by the owner of Ritual Salad & Apothecary.

business.note: Ritual Marketplace is a creative collective / retail space opened 2024. Searched Wisconsin DOR Retail Alcohol Beverage License Search for Superior WI — 1323 Broadway is listed as 'SPIRIT ROOM, THE' (JEFFERY WILLIAM HELLER), a separate bar tenant in the same building. Ritual Marketplace itself does not appear in the WI DOR license database, consistent with its character as a marketplace/event space without its own liquor license. Wisconsin DFI corporate search not accessed directly; no registration info found.

liquor.note: Marketplace / event venue. Not found as a licensee in WI DOR database for Superior WI. A separate entity (Spirit Room/Jeffery William Heller) holds a Class B license at 1323 Broadway — this appears to be a co-tenant bar, not Ritual Marketplace itself.

venues.name: Sacred Heart Music Center
venues.notable: Deconsecrated Roman Catholic cathedral built 1894-1896; served as seat of the Diocese of Duluth until 1957. Since 1985 operated as a nonprofit performance hall prized for its stunning acoustics and original Felgemaker pipe organ. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

business.note: Sacred Heart Music Center is a nonprofit performing arts organization. Not found on Duluth annual liquor licensee list. Searched duluthmn.gov Legistar and annual licensee PDFs. No liquor license expected for a concert hall of this type.

liquor.note: Nonprofit concert hall in a deconsecrated cathedral. No liquor license found in Duluth annual licensee lists. May obtain temporary on-sale permits for specific events.

venues.name: Sir Benedict's Tavern on the Lake
venues.notable: Beloved British-style tavern and sandwich shop near the Lakewalk on the east end of downtown. Known for hosting acoustic folk and trad music in an intimate room, often with waterside patio seating.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: SIR BENEDICTS IV, INC; DBA: SIR BENEDICT'S TAVERN ON THE LAKE

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: SIR BENEDICTS IV, INC. Also confirmed on 2016 Temporary On-Sale Intoxicating list (Legistar File 16-0410R).

venues.name: Spirit of the Lake Community Arts
venues.notable: Community arts nonprofit in the Lakeside neighborhood offering classes, exhibitions, and small-scale performances. Formerly operated as the Spirit of the Lake Community School.

business.note: Nonprofit community arts organization. Not found on Duluth annual liquor licensee list. Searched duluthmn.gov Legistar and annual licensee PDFs. No liquor license expected.

liquor.note: Community arts nonprofit. No liquor license found.

venues.name: Studio Four
venues.notable: Lower-level performance space at the Duluth Depot managed by the Minnesota Ballet; equipped as both a dance studio and a ~140-seat theater used for recitals, concerts, and festival programming.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); MINNESOTA BALLET listed under Theater licenses at 506 W Michigan St (the Duluth Depot building). Also HISTORIC UNION DEPOT, INC holds on-sale intoxicating for the building.
business.note: Studio Four is a performance space within the Depot building (506 W Michigan St). The on-sale intoxicating license for the Depot building is held by HISTORIC UNION DEPOT, INC. Minnesota Ballet holds a separate Theater license for its portion of the space.

liquor.license_type: on-sale intoxicating (under Depot building license held by HISTORIC UNION DEPOT, INC)
liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list; building license held by HISTORIC UNION DEPOT, INC; Minnesota Ballet holds Theater license separately

venues.name: The Moorish Room
venues.notable: Ornately decorated Moorish Revival room inside the 1925 Hotel Duluth (now Greysolon Plaza) off the main lobby. Originally the hotel's main restaurant; features hand-painted murals, chandeliers, and wall sconces. Operated by Greysolon Ballroom by Black Woods.

business.note: The Moorish Room is within the Greysolon Plaza / Hotel Duluth building at 231 E Superior St. Duluth's annual licensee list shows BLACK WATER AT GREYSOLON as licensee at this address (on-sale intoxicating). The Greysolon event spaces are operated by Black Woods Group.

venues.notable: Sports-bar-style tavern at the north end of Tower Avenue in Superior, with 14 TVs, extensive whiskey and tequila selections, pool, and darts.

venues.notable: Award-winning craft distillery in a Canal Park brick warehouse (formerly part of the Paulucci food empire) near the Aerial Lift Bridge, founded by Emily and Joel Vikre in 2013. Known for North Shore-inspired gins, aquavits, and whiskies.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); listed under Micro Distillery as VIKRE DISTILLERY, LLC; address: 525 LAKE AVE S STE 102

venues.name: West Theatre

capacity_source.method: venue-stated (Perfect Duluth Day 2019 opening article: 'The theater will seat 250 people')

venues.notable: Art Deco movie palace opened Christmas Day 1937, designed by architect Perry Crosier. After decades of closures and alternate uses, it was purchased in 2016 and restored as a movie theater and live-music/concert venue serving West Duluth's Spirit Valley business district.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); listed under Theaters as WEST THEATER, LLC; DBA: WEST THEATER

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: WEST THEATER, LLC; listed under Theater licenses

venues.notable: Wheeler Athletic Complex multi-use athletic field at 35th Ave W & Grand Ave in West Duluth; 2026 Homegrown Kickball Classic venue (relocated from Chester Bowl).

business.note: City of Duluth public athletic complex; no commercial business license expected.

liquor.note: City park / athletic field. Not on Duluth annual liquor licensee list.

venues.notable: Craft cidery taproom in the Lincoln Park Craft District serving natural ciders alongside Wrecktangle Pizza, Birdie hemp beverages, and Camp Creemee Vermont-style soft serve. Dog-friendly patio.

venues.name: Wussow's Concert Cafe
venues.notable: West Duluth coffeehouse and listening room in Spirit Valley (previously operated as Beaner's Central). Hosts local and touring acoustic, folk, and indie acts most nights.

business.note: The older Duluth licensee list (Legistar PDF 7514795) shows BEANER'S CENTRAL, INC at this address with an on-sale wine & beer license. As of 2024 the venue rebranded as Wussow's Concert Cafe; searched both Legistar PDFs and the venue is not listed under the new name. May be operating under a new entity or renewed under the Beaner's Central license with a DBA change. Status assessed as unknown pending confirmation of current license holder.

liquor.license_type: on-sale wine and beer (prior licensee: BEANER'S CENTRAL, INC)

venues.name: Zeitgeist Teatro
venues.notable: 120-seat performance theater inside the Zeitgeist Arts building (also home to Zeitgeist Zinema independent cinema and Zeitgeist Cafe/Bar) on East Superior Street. Home of the Renegade Theater Company (founded 1991). The full Zeitgeist Arts complex opened in 2009 in a restored historic downtown building.

business.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: ZEITGEIST ARTS, LLC; DBA: TEATRO/ZINEMA/ZA CAFÉ; address: 222 E SUPERIOR ST
business.note: License covers the entire Zeitgeist Arts complex including the Teatro theater, Zinema cinema, and ZA Café/Bar.

liquor.source: City of Duluth annual licensee list (Legistar PDF); legal entity: ZEITGEIST ARTS, LLC
